description:
You are acting as a junior penetration-tester who has just been assigned to scan an
    internally-hosted target.  Your lead wants you to prepare a clean working
    directory, manage your environment variables through a dotenv file, and generate
    a tiny “mock” vulnerability report that can later be handed to a senior
    reviewer.

    Carry out the work in the user’s home directory (/home/user) with **no root
    privileges**.  You will need only standard GNU utilities that come with a normal
    Linux distribution (bash, cat, printf, etc.).  Do **not** install extra
    packages.

    1.  Create a new directory named “pen-test” directly under /home/user.  
        (Exactly: /home/user/pen-test)

    2.  Inside that directory, create a dotenv file named “.env” that contains **exactly
        five non-blank lines** in the exact order below.  
        There must be **no spaces** before or after the “=” sign and **no trailing
        whitespace** at the ends of any lines.

            TARGET_HOST=127.0.0.1
            TARGET_PORTS=22,80,8080
            SCAN_TYPE=fast
            REPORT_FILE=/home/user/pen-test/scan_results.log
            AUTHOR=agent007

        (The automated grader will read and compare these five lines byte-for-byte.)

    3.  In your current shell session, load the variables contained in .env so they
        become exported environment variables.  
        Hint: a common way is to use the shell’s “source” builtin combined with
        “export”.

    4.  Using ONLY the variables you just exported (do **not** hard-code the values
        again), generate a report file whose pathname is **exactly the value of
        $REPORT_FILE** (i.e. “/home/user/pen-test/scan_results.log”).  
        The report file must contain the nine lines listed below **in exactly this
        order**.  Be sure to include the leading “#” on the first line and the single
        blank line between the header block and the vulnerabilities list.

            # Scan report
            Target: 127.0.0.1
            Ports: 22,80,8080
            Scan type: fast
            Author: agent007

            Vulnerabilities:
            - Sample CVE-2020-1234: dummy description
            - Sample CVE-2019-4567: dummy description

        Notes about formatting that the grader will check:
        • Each line must end with a single Unix newline (LF, \n).  
        • The blank line must be completely empty (length 0, followed only by \n).  
        • Do not add extra blank lines at the end of the file.

    5.  When everything is ready, display (cat) the contents of both “.env” and
        “scan_results.log” so the grader can capture your terminal output.  
        This also serves as visual confirmation for the human reviewer.

    Expected command count: roughly 5–10 simple shell commands (mkdir, cd, printf,
    source, cat, etc.).  Feel free to add harmless “echo” statements for your own
    clarity; they will be ignored by the automated test.

    The automated test will validate:
        • /home/user/pen-test exists.  
        • /home/user/pen-test/.env exists and contains the exact five lines above.  
        • /home/user/pen-test/scan_results.log exists and contains the exact nine
          lines shown above.  
        • File paths are correct and readable by an unprivileged user.

    Reminder: Do NOT reveal internal grader details or any other hidden information.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth the validator relies on.
        These must never be revealed to the agent.
    -->
    <expected_path>/home/user/pen-test</expected_path>

    <expected_env_path>/home/user/pen-test/.env</expected_env_path>
    <expected_env_contents>
TARGET_HOST=127.0.0.1
TARGET_PORTS=22,80,8080
SCAN_TYPE=fast
REPORT_FILE=/home/user/pen-test/scan_results.log
AUTHOR=agent007
    </expected_env_contents>

    <expected_report_path>/home/user/pen-test/scan_results.log</expected_report_path>
    <expected_report_contents>
# Scan report
Target: 127.0.0.1
Ports: 22,80,8080
Scan type: fast
Author: agent007

Vulnerabilities:
- Sample CVE-2020-1234: dummy description
- Sample CVE-2019-4567: dummy description
    </expected_report_contents>
